scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Medium Biofloc Establishment - Gujarat
page_title: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Medium Biofloc Establishment - Gujarat

details: The scheme “Medium Biofloc Establishment (25 tanks, 4-meter diameter and 1-meter height)” is a component under the “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)” scheme, implemented by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities. Under this scheme, various components of inland fisheries, including the establishment of Medium Biofloc and Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS) and freshwater hatcheries, are supported to accelerate inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies and boost inland fish production. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries.

Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits: The scheme provides financial assistance for various inland fisheries activities. For Medium Biofloc Establishment (25 tanks 4 meter diameter and 1 meter height), the unit cost is ₹25,00,000/- per unit.
The scale of assistance is as follows:
40% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less, is paid as subsidy.
Women, Scheduled Caste (SC), and Scheduled Tribe (ST) Categories:
60% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less, is paid as subsidy.
The assistance is funded through a 60% contribution from the Government of India and a 40% contribution from the State Government.
The assistance is provided only once in a lifetime to the beneficiary.
eligibility: The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.

The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.

Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

documents_required: Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport (as proof of identity)
Land Ownership Documents (if applicant has own private land)
Consent Form of Co-owners (in case of co-owned land)
Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Certificate (for Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries)

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scheme related to inland fisheries?
faqs.answer: Individuals, societies, self-help groups, associations, reservoir lease holders, and private entities involved in inland fisheries activities can apply for this scheme.

faqs.question: Is there any age limit for applying under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant must be above 18 years of age to be eligible for this scheme.

faqs.question: Can women and people from Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, all categories, including General, Women, Scheduled Castes, and Scheduled Tribes, are eligible to apply for the scheme.

faqs.question: Is land ownership necessary to get assistance under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant must have their own private land or land on a lease agreement of at least 10 years to be eligible for construction-related assistance.

faqs.question: If the land is co-owned, can I still apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a consent form from all co-owners must be submitted along with the application.

faqs.question: Where should I apply to get the benefits of this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants must register and apply online through the iKhedut portal at https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in .

faqs.question: Can I apply more than once under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, benefits under this scheme can be availed only once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Who is responsible for implementing this scheme in Gujarat?
faqs.answer: The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries under the State Government.

faqs.question: Do I need to have a bank account to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant must have their own bank account to be eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: What proof of identity is needed to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant must have Voter ID, Ration Card, Aadhaar Card, or Passport as proof of identity.

faqs.question: Are there any documents required for Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe applicants?
faqs.answer: Yes, SC and ST applicants must provide a valid Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe certificate.

faqs.question: Can people involved in inland fisheries without any group or organization apply individually?
faqs.answer: Yes, individuals involved in inland fisheries are also eligible to apply under the scheme.

faqs.question: How is the financial assistance under this scheme shared between governments?
faqs.answer: The assistance is paid through 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.

faqs.question: Can this scheme be used for fish farming in areas with limited land and water?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme supports fish farming through methods like Biofloc and RAS, which are suitable for areas with less land and water.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of setting up freshwater hatcheries under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The purpose is to increase inland fish production capacity through the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
